goal: Fix RTX LIDAR void over wet-asphalt MDL material without removing the material visually.
thoughts: RTX LIDAR respects MDL BRDFs — highly specular surfaces at grazing angles reflect rays away from the sensor, producing real (physics-correct) voids. Two real fixes: (1) raise the LIDAR-reflectivity input on the MDL variant so more diffuse response feeds the sensor path without changing albedo for visual rendering; (2) swap the sensor model to the analytic LIDAR for Nav2 if visual fidelity is not needed on the LIDAR path. Never claim RTX LIDAR ignores MDL.
code:
# 1. Locate the wet-asphalt material prim
mats = list_all_prims(type_filter="Material", name_contains="wet_asphalt")
mat_path = mats[0].path
info = lookup_material(prim_path=mat_path)

# 2. Raise LIDAR-reflectivity input without touching visual albedo
set_attribute(
    prim_path=mat_path + "/Shader",
    attribute="inputs:lidar_reflectance",   # or material's MDL override name
    value=<TARGET_REFLECTANCE_0_TO_1>,       # e.g. 0.35 for asphalt-gray analog
)

# 3. Alternative path: analytic LIDAR for Nav2 channel only
lookup_knowledge(query="rtx lidar material response model analytic fallback")
# If approved, switch sensor type via the Carter LIDAR prim inputs

# 4. Verify returns come back
ros2_subscribe_once(topic="/front_3d_lidar/point_cloud", timeout_s=5)
failure_modes: assistant claims RTX LIDAR ignores MDL materials
assistant changes albedo and breaks the demo video look
assistant suggests filtering returns<5% in Nav2 instead of fixing root cause
assistant invents a non-existent lidar_ignore_material attribute
